chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D - 1008 of 2026 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Priority 1. For orders on office objection Nos.1 and 2 2. For orders on CMA No.4159/2026 3. For hearing of main case 02.04.2026 M/s. Farooq Ahmed Naek and Syed Qaim A. Shah, Advocates for the petitioner. Syed Manzoor Ali Spl. Prosecutor NAB along with Waliullah, I.O./Deputy Director NAB. Ms. Shahzia Hanjrah, DAG. ------------------------------------ Through this petition, the petitioner has challenged the order dated 31.01.2026 passed by learned Accountability Court No.IV, Sindh Karachi, whereby two separate applications filed by the petitioner for depositing the amount of affectees in Reference No.11 of 2020 was declined. It is the case of the petitioner that NAB filed Reference against the petitioner for fraud in booking of a project named “Gulshan-e-Sahal” and after investigation Reference for an amount of Rs.547.100 Million was filed before the Accountability Court. During the instigation of the Reference, an application under Section 25(b) of the National Accountability Ordinance for plea of bargain was filed by the petitioner, which was accepted by the NAB and approved by the trial Court. The petitioner through two different applications moved the trial Court for settlement of the claim of the alleged affectees. The National Accountability Bureau did not oppose the applications, however, learned trial Court refused to entertain such applications. During proceedings learned Special Prosecutor NAB filed a statement before the trial Court, which reads as under: “1.

The National Accountability Bureau did not oppose the applications, however, learned trial Court refused to entertain such applications. During proceedings learned Special Prosecutor NAB filed a statement before the trial Court, which reads as under: “1. With Reference to an application filed by Pirano Khan Sahal for payment of entire liability already accepted vide order dated 28.01.2025 and subsequent application submitted by him for provision of Bank Accounts details of Chairman NAB and in supersession of earlier reply filed by NAB to the last application, the mater has been examined by the HQ. The competent authority has endorsed the applicant’s request and the approval is communicated accordingly. 2. The bank accounts details are as under:- Chairman NAB – Cheating Public at Large National Bank of Pakistan PM Secretariat Branch, Islamabad. Therefore, the application filed by Pirano Sahal and fixed for order on 27.01.2026 may kindly be disposed with direction to him to pay the already determined amount to the tune of Rs.644.5 Million through Pay Order in the name of Chairman NAB.” Today learned Special Prosecutor NAB has consented for the grant of instant petition. Accordingly, by consent of the parties this petition is disposed of. The order dated 31.01.2026 is hereby set-aside. The applications filed by the petitioner is granted. The petitioner shall deposit the amount for payment of the alleged affectees in the bank account of the Chairman NAB, which shall be disbursed to the affectees after due identification and verification in presence of the petitioner or his nominee. The petition along with listed application stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE Azeem
